COT Trade Ledger —

Official backtest ↗ Prediction-bias backtest ↗

Every trade at the selected risk : reward bracket (stop / take-profit set below, 5-bar time stop). Left = forecast (the Wednesday nowcast's predicted composite); right = official (the released report's composite). Same audited fill engine as the two backtest reports (real-print entries, same-bar SL-first, gap-through fills at the open, lumber roll gaps removed). Each week is one card, colored by the combined net; the running cumulative for each side is in every card header, newest week on top so the grand totals sit here. Gross of costs, sign-only sizing, non-compounded (each trade = a fixed $100k leg, so 1% = $1,000). This is a diagnostic ledger, not a promise of weekly profit — see the backtests for why.

Realism verdicts (full-sweep study, 2026-07-12, net of per-market costs): the default here — risk 1% / reward 5% — is the only bracket shape with non-negative net expectancy (+0.014R/trade at a 5-bar stop, t=0.37; still statistically zero). Expect most exits to be stops or time-outs, not targets: at this bracket the TP fills only ~8% (5-bar) to ~14% (10-bar) of trades — the far target is a tail-catcher, not the routine exit. Tested and rejected: scale-out ladders (thirds at 1/2.5/5% → −0.10R/trade, t=−3.6 — partial exits sell the only tranche that pays while losers still take the full stop; big accounts should scale position size, never ladder exits) and removing the time stop (hold-to-resolution ≈ −0.004R/trade — the edge decays monotonically after the first week: +0.014 → +0.010 → −0.004 at 5/10/20-bar stops). Inverted brackets (small target, wide stop) are the worst thing tested: TP0.25/SL5 = −0.09R/trade at t=−16 despite an 85% win rate.
